title: Death Renewal ghosting graphically intense thread
display_title: Death Renewal ghosting graphically intense thread
wikitext:
[]: Death Renewal ghosting (graphically intense thread)             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Oct 27, 2006 11:45 am Brought to you from land of broadbands. (Split from The Kaworu-Katsuragi Connection Thread ) I don't like tooting my horn all that much, but there's just no way you're fanwanking your way out of this one ;) The first thing to keep in mind is that EVERY SINGLE ONE of these screenshots is a very first or very last frame from a "shot". These have a much bigger chance of being neglected in the cleanup process, don't ask me why, they just do. If they had reused old cels, it shouldn't look this wobbly and dirty, so I guess it's because even when remastering for Renewal (and doing a great job, really) they couldn't fix it when, at the biginning and end of EVERY SINGLE SHOT, the picture is skewing and slipping in and out of the screen. See for yourself how wobbly it is. I animated some of these as to easier locate the anomaly. The one that started everything (00:01:51) Detail (00:04:34) Detail (00:05:34) Detail (00:06:12) Detail (00:06:14) (00:06:24) Detail (00:06:32) Detail (00:07:23) Detail (00:07:51) (00:07:57) Detail (00:08:06) (00:08:33) (00:09:09) (00:09:26) (00:10:27) Detail (00:12:49) Detail (00:13:52) (00:16:30) (00:17:34) (00:18:11) Detail (00:21:11) Detail (00:21:18) Detail (00:21:25) (00:21:26) Detail (00:21:38) Detail (00:22:09) Detail (00:22:14) (00:22:14) (00:22:50) Detail (00:22:52) (00:22:54) (00:22:59) (00:24:34) Detail (00:26:48) Detail (00:29:54) Detail (00:30:38) Detail (00:32:19) (00:32:20) (00:32:22) Detail (00:32:23) (00:33:24) Detail (00:33:52) (00:34:11) (00:34:25) Detail (00:34:48) Detail (00:35:00) (00:35:09) (00:35:33) (00:35:35) (00:35:51) (00:36:05) Detail (00:36:19) (00:36:19) Detail (00:36:21) (00:36:22) (00:36:23) (00:36:25) (00:36:30) Detail (00:36:32) (00:36:51) (00:36:53) (00:37:34) ITT, Shinji has a cunt (00:37:45) Detail (00:38:15) (00:39:37) These dirty frames are at their most dirty at the fast-paced parts. The cleaners probably just lost focus by then, much like I gradually did, retracing their lapses. This is all, of course, assuming that my theory is right. But there is a possibility that I might be wrong. No, wait a minute... There isn't. This is an undisputable pattern. So it's obviously established that the "Tree of Life-ghosting" is linked to these dirty frames. And seeing how some look just like it, some resemble it, and some are nothing like it, I think it's safe to submit that there's no wanking this one off as a fit of genius. Even if the first appearance it IS about the only instance were it occurs in the UPPER edge of the screen. The last thing to keep in mind is that this was all done in one night, in one sitting, and by one pair of tired eyes, so statistics should indicate that I've probably missed at least 25% of noteworthy frames. And I only scanned two thirds of the movie. Finally, there's something that came up while I was doing this, and I though I might ruin the mood for you too. There's a small, pubic looking piece of hair, stuck on the right side of the screen, clearly visable during a lot of the string quartet segments. You'll see it. For further insight, here's a package with ALL the screenies I kept. (5,33MB) [url]http://rapidshare.com/files/906582/DeathDirt.rar.html[/url] Motherfucker! Did this thing ever take long to compose. Hope it cleared some things up.

[]:             Quote Post by Lujerker » Fri Oct 27, 2006 11:55 am for that reason kaworu loves Shinji ....

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 27, 2006 12:04 pm Mmmm... Interesting. The Shinji camel-toe is something that would confirm a lot of suspicions.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Oct 27, 2006 12:23 pm OK, I know when to surrender: the 2I TOL in Death is officially defunct, and you won't ever see me mention it again. Props to Kaysow for setting us straight...

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Fri Oct 27, 2006 12:32 pm Oh my God. WIN.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Oct 27, 2006 12:33 pm Great Success! Sorry if I sounded too self-righteous there. But I DID spend 10 hours preparing this post =)

[]:             Quote Post by Lujerker » Fri Oct 27, 2006 12:38 pm Answer: the animators don't be perfect.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Oct 27, 2006 4:28 pm You win. I know exactly why they can't clean those also, what you are seeing is the literal glue that holds the film together. To fix it, they'd have to digitally edit the affected frames. That's why they show up between the cuts.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Fri Oct 27, 2006 5:33 pm I must also quickly add: "I BE STROKIN'!"

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Oct 27, 2006 8:37 pm Kaysow wrote: But I DID spend 10 hours preparing this post =)

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Oct 27, 2006 9:29 pm It's not just animation, it's most things shot on film, especially when made under tight dead lines. I dunno why I never thought of this though, I've seen it in my own films...

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Fri Oct 27, 2006 9:46 pm My favorite one is the one with shinjis accident! 3rd impact in your pants! :o

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 29, 2006 12:44 pm Shin-seiki wrote: OK, I know when to surrender: the 2I TOL in Death is officially defunct, and you won't ever see me mention it again.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Oct 29, 2006 5:54 pm The Reichu is alive, ooh-rah! My guess is that the wobbling has something to do with NGE being film-based rather than digital animation; they glue pieces together and then run them through some kind of copying system. If the film-to-sprocket fit is a little loose, the image will wobble. At least, that's what I remember. I'll have to ask my brother when he gets back from fencing, since he does a lot of work with filmstuffs.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Oct 29, 2006 6:40 pm This thread is awesome because it gets rid of unnecessary baseless theories and fanwanking trying to explain "HOLY SHIT WHY IS THE ToL THERE".

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Oct 29, 2006 9:25 pm MaggotMaster wrote: This thread is awesome because it gets rid of unnecessary baseless theories and fanwanking trying to explain "HOLY SHIT WHY IS THE ToL THERE".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 31, 2006 10:52 am MaggotMaster wrote: This thread is awesome because it gets rid of unnecessary baseless theories and fanwanking trying to explain "HOLY SHIT WHY IS THE ToL THERE".

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Oct 31, 2006 2:52 pm Had that been the ToL, what difference would it have made? Really.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Tue Oct 31, 2006 4:19 pm Because in EoE, Fuyutsuki says something along the lines that the ToL has been "reformed", implying that it was already around at some point prior to EoE. Having it be involved in 2I kinda answers that, but if it's not, then it's left completely unanswered.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 31, 2006 9:02 pm felineki wrote: Because in EoE, Fuyutsuki says something along the lines that the ToL has been "reformed", implying that it was already around at some point prior to EoE. Having it be involved in 2I kinda answers that, but if it's not, then it's left completely unanswered.
